name: Shecky Greene
abstract: Shecky Greene (born Fred Sheldon Greenfield; April 8, 1926 – December 31, 2023) was an American comedian and actor. He was known for his nightclub performances in Las Vegas, Nevada, where he became a headliner in the 1950s and 1960s.

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: Then there was Shecky Greene; he was always a comedian. I used to love those shows because the band only had 28 minutes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: Shecky said to me one night, he says, “I’ve walked out on several comics that were too dirty for me.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022
